feedingProfile.preferredNpk: balanced (e.g. 10-10-10) at planting, then monthly higher-phosphorus or high-potassium bloom feed

commonIssues: Highly susceptible to powdery mildew; water at the base, never overhead, and ensure good air circulation (UMN, Clemson).
Also prone to Alternaria blight, bacterial leaf spot, root rots, Botrytis and damping-off in cold wet conditions; slugs damage young plants (Missouri Botanical Garden, RHS).
Overwatering and wet foliage promote fungal disease; only water when the top 2.5-5 cm of soil are dry (UMN, Clemson).
Frost-tender warm-season annual: do not plant out until soil reaches ~21C and all frost danger has passed; killed by frost (UMN, Clemson, RHS).

Tall cultivars (60-120 cm) benefit from netting or support secured at ~30-40 cm above ground; dwarf cultivars are best for containers — extension guidance notes zinnias perform better in beds than containers (RHS, UMN).
Deadhead spent blooms to extend flowering and pinch young plants for a bushier habit (Clemson, Missouri Botanical Garden).

source.citation: University of Minnesota Extension — Zinnia. Full sun 8+ hours/day in a very warm location; water only when the top 1-2 inches of soil are dry; performs better in garden beds than containers.
University of Minnesota Extension — Zinnia. 'Only water when the top 1 to 2 inches of soil are dry'; avoid overwatering and wet foliage, which promote fungal disease (powdery mildew).

University of Minnesota Extension — Zinnia. General-purpose fertilizer (equal N-P-K) at planting, then monthly applications of a higher-phosphorus formula to promote new blooms (basis for baseIntervalDays=30 and preferredNpk).

University of Minnesota Extension — Zinnia. Direct seeding requires soil temperature of at least 70F (21C); transplants acceptable at 60F (16C); germination 70-75F; frost-tender annual planted after last frost.

Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC — How to Grow Zinnias: The Best Varieties & Care Tips. Full sun (6+ hours); about 1 inch (25 mm) of water per week applied at the base, watering when the top 1-2 inches of soil feel dry; sow after last frost with soil/air around 70F; spacing 8-24 inches; powdery mildew, Alternaria and bacterial leaf spot.

Clemson Cooperative Extension HGIC — How to Grow Zinnias. Full sun, minimum 6 hours of direct sun (basis for minSunHours=6); deadheading extends the bloom season; suitable for containers using dwarf cultivars.

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Zinnia elegans (Kemper Code a618). Annual grown in USDA zones 2-11; full sun; medium water; humusy, evenly moist, well-drained soils; height 1-4 ft, spread 0.75-1 ft, upright bushy habit; blooms June to frost; plant after last frost date; susceptible to powdery mildew, leaf spots, root rots, blight, Botrytis and Japanese beetles; pinch young plants for compact bushy form.

Missouri Botanical Garden Plant Finder — Zinnia elegans. Upright, branching annual, not a climber or trailer (basis for growthHabit=upright and low verticalSuitability); disease-resistant cultivars recommended in hot, humid climates.

RHS Growing Guide — How to grow zinnia. Half-hardy annual that won't tolerate cold; plant out only after all frost danger (normally June in the UK); sow at 20C and grow on at 15-18C; keep watered in hot dry weather without wetting foliage; container plants benefit from a high-potassium feed (e.g. tomato feed) while flowering, in-ground plants need no supplemental feeding; tall varieties supported by netting secured ~30-40 cm above ground; alternaria blight, botrytis, damping-off and slugs are the main problems.

RHS Growing Guide — How to grow zinnia. Container-grown zinnias fed during flowering with high-potassium feed; supports containerLeachAdjustment and the bloom-feed half of preferredNpk; support guidance for tall cultivars (requiresSupport stays false because typical/dwarf container cultivars are self-supporting).

Estimated/conservative values not stated verbatim by any source: baseIntervalDays=3 translates Clemson/UMN '~1 inch per week, water when top 1-2 in of soil are dry' into a practical check interval (containers dry faster than beds); hotWeatherMultiplier=1.5 and rainSkipThresholdMm=12 (~0.5 in) are conservative defaults derived from the 1-inch/week guidance; coldHardyMinC=5 and heatStressC=38 are inferred warm-season-annual bounds (sources state >=21C soil preferred, frost-killed, very heat tolerant) rather than published thresholds; container volumes (4 L min ~ a 20 cm pot for one dwarf plant, 8-20 L ideal) are converted from common pot sizes — extension sources confirm container suitability for dwarf cultivars but publish no explicit volume; plantingWindowsByZone are illustrative after-last-frost windows (zone 5 ~May-Jun, zone 7 ~Apr-Jun).
